# .claude-memory

This directory holds Claude Code's auto memory files, symlinked from
`~/.claude/projects/<encoded-project>/memory/`.

## Why this exists

Claude Code stores auto memory at a fixed path under `~/.claude/` with no
configuration option to change the location. In ephemeral environments
(e.g. Docker containers) this directory gets destroyed between sessions.

By symlinking the memory directory into the git repo, the memory files
persist with the repository and survive container rebuilds.

## How it works

The `claude_code.memory.link [project_dir]` shell function:
1. Creates `.claude-memory/memory/` in the project directory (defaults to repo root)
2. Symlinks `~/.claude/projects/<encoded-project>/memory/` to point here
3. Generates this README

## Reference

- https://docs.claude.com/en/docs/memory

## Note

This file is auto-generated by `claude_code.memory.link`. Do not edit manually.
